pages.raw_text:
While the nationwide movement
to boycott all meats until the pres
ent high prices are cut down by
the trusts, Is receiving much dis
cusslon here, there has been no
movement to initiate starvation
strike" in Spokane.


Much to the Joy Of the vege-
tartan cafes, number of individ-
uals have decided that they will
keep away from the flesh pots until
tenderloins and sirloins take tum-
BILE. But the general boycott, such
as those which have been started
in other cities, has not been in
augurated.


The matter was not given con-
sideration at the meetings of any
of the labor unions last night. 1t
is possible that it may be discussed
at the meeting of Federal labor
Agent w. A. Gift of that organiza-
tion said today: ~ am In favor of
the boycott; it will probably /
discussed at our meeting tonightP
STATEMENTS FROM UNIONS


Business Agent Chavez of Fei
eral union 12,222 and seven mem
bers who were interviewed at their
local hall, said that they were in
favor of the boycott; but it was in
timated that some of the number
hoped that they would not be OBJ
liged to abstain from meat very
long.


Business Agent Barber of the
plumbers, steam and gas filters
said: Il am for it. l believe it
is good way to bring them to
terms
